notes: README.md is the sole canonical human-facing project guide in Chinese.
project.json records repo-local operational contracts that should stay aligned with README.md.
Live tokens, callback URLs containing tokens, and human passwords must never be committed.

openaiOAuthAccountImport.purpose: document the canonical import and validation contract for OpenAI OAuth accounts
parsingRules: Treat a single OAuth login event as one indivisible token batch.
Reject mixed token bundles even if individual tokens appear valid.
Use decoded id_token claims as the source of truth for OpenAI account identity metadata.
If email, plan_type, chatgpt_account_id, chatgpt_user_id, or organization_id are omitted, import may backfill them from id_token.
If those fields are supplied explicitly, they should match the decoded id_token claims.
Do not use a human password or bare email address as the OpenAI OAuth import credential.
validationWorkflow: write or import the account with one internally consistent token bundle
require a real successful upstream response before treating the account as healthy
if a stale Fail state remains, run POST /api/v1/admin/accounts/:id/clear-error
knownFailurePatterns: Mismatched id_token and access_token from different login batches cause false repair loops.
A previous Fail state can persist visually until clear-error is called after a successful retest.

requiredEvidence: README.md and project.json describe the same import contract
no plaintext secrets are introduced into tracked files
